extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never both true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires the conjunction of all 10 conditions including heart_failure. rec_drug_b is set true by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever heart_failure is true. Since the conjunction for rec_drug_a includes heart_failure, any input that satisfies the conjunction necessarily also has heart_failure true, which fires the rec_drug_b trigger. There are no competing/higher-priority rules for either output, and defaults only apply to unset outputs (both are explicitly set true here). Therefore setting all 10 conjunction inputs to true (asthma/seizure_history irrelevant, set false) makes rec_drug_a=true AND rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion.
